trivia_bullets.text: Scrounging Bandar originated in Aether Revolt (January 2017), the second set of the Kaladesh block. 'Bandar' is the Kaladeshi word for monkey — the plane's Cat Monkey creatures are named using that term. Aether Revolt depicts the renegade uprising against the Consulate on the steampunk-aether plane of Kaladesh (later renamed Avishkar), home plane of Chandra Nalaar. [Scryfall; WebSearch: Aether Revolt lore summary]

Flavor text: "It was right here a second ago . . ." [Scryfall flavor_text field]. The speaker is unnamed. The quote captures the Bandar's scrounging, thieving character — something was just here and now it isn't, attributable to the creature.

The counter-transfer ability — moving +1/+1 counters from the Bandar to any other creature at each upkeep — makes it a counter-distribution engine in decks that want counters elsewhere (Infect, Proliferate, counters-matter commanders). EDHREC rank 8572 places it in the moderate fringe; it sees more play in focused counter-synergy builds than generalist green decks. [Scryfall edhrec_rank field; EDHREC card page]

Printed in Aether Revolt (2017), Jumpstart (2020), Commander Legends (2020), and Jumpstart 2022 (2022) — four total printings. Illustrated by Shreya Shetty, who had approximately 15 MTG cards to her credit as of the Aether Revolt era. The Cat Monkey creature type is a rare combination; the card is one of the few to define what that creature type looks like in art. [Scryfall prints search; Gatherer artist search via WebSearch]
